title: Bloom Energy Q1 2026 Analysis
text: Bloom Energy delivered exceptional Q1 2026 earnings with EPS of $0.44 crushing the $0.1285 consensus estimate, a 242% beat. Product revenue surged 208% year-over-year to $653.3 million, while total revenue climbed 130%. The company's transformation into an AI power provider is materializing. Management raised full-year 2026 guidance substantially: revenue $3.4B-$3.8B representing 80% YoY growth at midpoint, non-GAAP EPS $1.85-$2.25, and adjusted EBITDA $650M-$800M. CEO KR Sridhar stated that Bloom is rapidly becoming the standard and go-to choice for on-site power. Two major deals anchor growth: a $5 billion collaboration with Brookfield Asset Management and an Oracle partnership involving a 2.8 GW commitment for powering AI data centers. Related-party sales to Brookfield JVs reached $373.3 million versus $2.8 million prior year, signaling customer concentration that warrants tracking as deals diversify. With a $20 billion backlog, execution on capacity and hyperscaler conversions remains critical.
